name: rasa.domain.soccer.goalkeeping
description: RasaOS Domain · Soccer Goalkeeping. Coaching foundation for soccer goalkeeping — technique, shot-stopping, distribution, positioning, training drills, match preparation, and keeper development across age/level.

rasa.note: Structural / reference domain. v0.2.0 ships a foundational goalkeeping knowledge core — PHILOSOPHY + framework/ (development, methodology, communication) + syllabus/ (nine technical categories) + drills/ (the named-drill library) + vocabulary — synthesized from three real curricula (see content/reference/). This is the foundation for everything in the domain; a future toolkit layer (session-builder, per-keeper development tracker, drill-selection skills) sits on top in content/skills/. Forked from domain-core v1.1.0.
rasa.sources_policy: Source PDFs are third-party copyrighted; held locally by the maintainer (gitignored), cited in content/reference/sources/SOURCES.md, NOT redistributed in this repo.

sources.title: Developing a Training Program for Coaching Goalkeeping (The GK Trainer)
sources.author: Rob Walker, US National Goalkeeping Staff Coach
sources.anchors: technical syllabus, the two training methods, U12-U19 load bands

sources.title: ECNL Goalkeeping Curriculum & Handbook, First Edition
sources.author: Birmingham United Soccer Association (BUSA)
sources.anchors: philosophy, four-stage communication, tactical situations, mentality

sources.title: Goalkeeper Plan 2015-2016
sources.author: youth-club GK program
sources.anchors: organization, U9-U18 age syllabus, named drill library, testing

rasa.naming: FIRST sub-namespaced domain (canon precedent, 2026-06-25). Canonical name rasa.domain.soccer.goalkeeping uses a dotted soccer.<specialty> hierarchy — valid because the schema ElementName first-party identifier is '.+'. Folder + repo use the dashed form (domain-soccer-goalkeeping); folder↔name is intentionally NOT reconstructable here, so tooling must read the dotted name from rasa.json#name (refresh-registry does). Alias rasa.domain.soccer-goalkeeping retained for flat-form discoverability. Future siblings under this hierarchy: rasa.domain.soccer.<striking|tactics|fitness|...>.

element.description: Files mirrored into the install target per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. The goalkeeping core (PHILOSOPHY, framework/, syllabus/, drills/, vocabulary) installs to .claude/goalkeeping/ (file-replace + directory-mirror; Element-owned, refreshed on upgrade). content/README.md + content/reference/ (the three source PDFs + provenance) ship opt-in (authoring/provenance, not auto-installed). The empty skills/rules/agents scaffold dirs remain opt-in placeholders for a future toolkit layer. The v1.1.0 output-style-enforcement files ship file-replace (inert until the consumer opts in) plus opt-in docs.

files.note: Domain-core overview + map (replaces the fork's SHAPE.md, now deleted). Authoring doc; not installed into consumer projects.
The synthesized coaching philosophy — the manifesto the framework + syllabus express. Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.
Goalkeeping glossary — technique terms, the four-stage communication commands, methodology terms. Referenced by every core file.

Development spine: development-framework (age/level model + per-stage testing), training-methodology (Simple-to-Complex / Whole-Part-Whole + session structure + organization + charting), communication-syllabus (the four-stage command ladder). Plus the framework README index.

The technical/tactical curriculum — nine categories (handling-catching, footwork-movement, shot-stopping-diving, aerial-crosses, one-v-one-breakaway, distribution, positioning-angles, playing-with-feet, psychology-mentality) each with progressions, cues, faults, stage emphasis, related drills + terms. Plus the syllabus README index.
The drill library — named exercises as cards grouped by syllabus area, cross-linked to the syllabus + training methodology. Plus the drills README index.

Provenance: reference/README.md + reference/sources/SOURCES.md CITE the three source curricula. The source PDFs are third-party copyrighted, held locally by the maintainer (gitignored under reference/sources/), and are NOT redistributed in this (public) repo. opt-in; not auto-installed.
Toolkit-shape scaffold subdir. Forks that adopt the toolkit pattern populate this and change policy to directory-mirror. Structural-shape forks delete this entry + the directory.
Toolkit-shape scaffold subdir. Forks adopting toolkit add rule .md files here + change policy. Note canonical install target is .claude/ root (rules install flat per domain-code precedent).
Toolkit-shape scaffold subdir for Claude subagent definitions. Forks adopting toolkit add agent .md files here + change policy to directory-mirror.
How-to for the 3-layer response-format enforcement pattern (output style + CLAUDE.md pointer + opt-in Stop hook). Fork-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

Stop hook — the 'hard' layer. Installed but INERT until the consumer wires it via settings (see settings.snippet.json) AND creates .claude/output-style.enforce.json. Element-owned generic code; refreshed on Element upgrade.
Example enforcement config + per-session toggle template. Copy to .claude/output-style.enforce.json to turn the Stop hook ON; delete that copy to turn it OFF.
settings.json Stop-hook registration snippet. Doc only; the consumer merges it once by hand. Not installed automatically.

seed.description: One-time files copied at install only. Per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7.

files.note: Per-project Claude session contract. Lives in install-target root; project-owned forever. Placeholder-free (Element identity lives in .claude/rasa.lock.json — current install-policy taxonomy doesn't substitute placeholders in skip-if-exists files; flagged in v0.1.0 AUDIT).

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). Substitutes {{ELEMENT_NAME}}, {{ELEMENT_KIND}}, {{ELEMENT_VERSION}}, {{ELEMENT_REPO}}, {{ELEMENT_BRANCH}}, {{ELEMENT_SHA}}, {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init's init-only-with-sha handler.

Starter response-format output style — the 'soft' layer (v1.1.0). Copied once; project owns it. Select with /output-style rasa-default or outputStyle in settings. Placeholder-free per the documented skip-if-exists policy gap.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init. v1.0.0 ships none — forks add as needed.
